description: Eval tests for /rune:brainstorm multi-mode brainstorming skill

evals.prompt: I want to run /rune:brainstorm for 'add real-time notifications'. Walk me through what happens: (1) How does mode selection work? (2) What are the 3 modes and their differences? (3) What does Phase 0 Clarity Check do? (4) How does brainstorm auto-detection prevent duplicate sessions?
evals.expected_output: Mode selection walkthrough: (1) Parses $ARGUMENTS for --quick (Solo) and --deep (Deep) flags. If neither flag, presents AskUserQuestion with 3 options. (2) Three modes: Solo (pure Q&A, no agents, fastest), Roundtable (3 advisor agents: user-advocate, tech-realist, devils-advocate), Deep (advisors + up to 3 elicitation sages). (3) Clarity Check assesses whether brainstorming is needed — clear signals (acceptance criteria, specific patterns, constrained scope) suggest skipping to /rune:devise; brainstorm signals (vague terms, multiple interpretations, undiscussed trade-offs) proceed. (4) Auto-detection: scans docs/brainstorms/*.md, tmp/plans/*/brainstorm-decisions.md, tmp/brainstorm-*/brainstorm-decisions.md for slug matches; also checks echo search for related entries. Recency filter: skip files older than 90 days. Always AskUserQuestion to confirm reuse.
expectations: Lists 3 modes: Solo, Roundtable, Deep
Explains --quick forces Solo, --deep forces Deep
Describes AskUserQuestion with 3 options for mode selection
Lists 3 advisor agents: user-advocate, tech-realist, devils-advocate
Describes Clarity Check: clear signals vs brainstorm signals
Explains brainstorm auto-detection with slug matching
Notes 90-day recency filter for brainstorm reuse
Notes AskUserQuestion always required for reuse confirmation (never auto-use)

evals.prompt: Explain the Team Bootstrap phase for brainstorm. What is the teamTransition protocol? What are the 6 steps? How does session isolation work with the state file?
evals.expected_output: Team Bootstrap (Phase 1, Team/Deep modes only): The teamTransition protocol is a standard 6-step pattern. Step 1: Validate timestamp (SEC-001, exactly 13 digits). Step 2: TeamDelete retry-with-backoff (4 attempts: 0s, 3s, 6s, 10s). Step 3: Filesystem fallback gated on !teamDeleteSucceeded (rm team/task dirs via CHOME pattern). Step 4: TeamCreate with 'Already leading' catch-and-recover. Step 5: Post-create verification (read config.json). Step 6: Write state file tmp/.rune-brainstorm-{timestamp}.json with session isolation fields: config_dir, owner_pid, session_id, feature, mode. Workspace created with subdirs: rounds/, advisors/, research/, elicitation/, design/. Signal directory created for fast completion detection.
expectations: Lists all 6 steps of teamTransition protocol
Notes timestamp validation: exactly 13 digits (SEC-001)
Notes TeamDelete retry delays: 0s, 3s, 6s, 10s
Explains filesystem fallback gated on !teamDeleteSucceeded
Describes 'Already leading' catch-and-recover
Lists state file fields: config_dir, owner_pid, session_id
Lists workspace subdirectories: rounds, advisors, research, elicitation, design
Mentions signal directory for fast completion detection

evals.prompt: Describe the Understanding Rounds phase in detail. How do rounds work in Solo vs Team/Deep mode? What are the 4 round topics? When does the system advance phases?
evals.expected_output: Understanding Rounds (Phase 2): Solo mode: Lead asks questions directly via AskUserQuestion (one at a time). Team/Deep mode: Lead orchestrates advisor rounds — sends context to each advisor via SendMessage, waits for responses (signal files + TaskList fallback, advisorTimeoutMs default 90s), curates inputs, presents to user. 4 round topics: Round 1 Understanding (purpose, users, motivation), Round 2 Approaches (proposals, challenges, trade-offs), Round 3 Refinement (edge cases, constraints, priorities), Round 4 Convergence (optional — final alignment, open concerns). Max 4 rounds (configurable via talisman misc.brainstorm.max_rounds). Advance when idea is clear OR user says 'proceed'. Word limit: 300 words per advisor (configurable). YAGNI applied throughout.
expectations: Distinguishes Solo mode (AskUserQuestion) from Team/Deep mode (advisor rounds)
Lists 4 round topics with descriptions
Notes Round 4 is optional
Mentions advisorTimeoutMs default 90s
Notes max 4 rounds from talisman config
Notes 300-word limit per advisor contribution
Mentions YAGNI principle
Describes advance condition: idea clear OR user says 'proceed'

evals.prompt: Explain Phase 3.5 Design Asset Detection. How does Figma URL scanning work? What SSRF protections exist? When is the user prompted for a Figma URL?
evals.expected_output: Design Asset Detection (Phase 3.5, all modes): Scans feature description, selected approach, AND round transcripts for Figma URLs using FIGMA_URL_PATTERN regex. SSRF defense: extracted URLs are filtered through SSRF_BLOCKLIST (localhost, 127.x, 0.0.0.0, private ranges 10.x, 192.168.x, 172.16-31.x, link-local 169.254.x, IPv6 ::1 and ::ffff:127, reserved TLDs .local/.internal/.corp/.test/.example/.invalid/.localhost). URLs must also contain 'figma.com' to pass. When Figma URL found: sets design_sync_candidate=true, appends Design Assets section. When no URL but design keywords detected (figma, design, mockup, wireframe, prototype, ui kit, etc): prompts user via AskUserQuestion.
expectations: Notes scanning covers feature description, approach, AND round transcripts
Describes FIGMA_URL_PATTERN for URL extraction
Lists SSRF blocklist entries: localhost, 127.x, private ranges, IPv6
Notes URLs must contain 'figma.com' to pass validation
Explains design_sync_candidate flag when Figma URL found
Describes DESIGN_KEYWORD_PATTERN trigger for user prompt
Notes AskUserQuestion used when keywords detected but no URL

evals.prompt: Walk me through the Elicitation Sages phase (Phase 4). When does it trigger? How are sage count and methods selected? How do sages integrate with the brainstorm team?
evals.expected_output: Elicitation Sages (Phase 4, Deep mode only): Triggered only in Deep mode after advisor rounds complete. Sage count determined by keyword fan-out: 15 brainstorm-specific keywords (architecture, security, risk, design, trade-off, migration, performance, decision, approach, comparison, breaking-change, auth, api, complex, novel-approach). Hits >= 4: 3 sages; >= 2: 2 sages; else: 1 sage. Methods scored and assigned from methods.csv (plan:0 phase). Sages spawned on the SAME rune-brainstorm-{timestamp} team via Agent() with run_in_background. Tasks created per sage. Outputs merged into brainstorm document after TaskList-based polling completion.
expectations: Notes Deep mode only
Lists keyword count thresholds: >= 4 (3 sages), >= 2 (2 sages), else 1
Notes 15 brainstorm-specific keywords (wider than forge/review)
Explains methods assigned from methods.csv
Notes sages join the SAME team (not a separate team)
Describes TaskList-based polling for completion
Mentions outputs merged into brainstorm document

evals.prompt: Explain the Quality Gate (Phase 5). What are the 7 dimensions? How do they map to quality tiers? How does Advisor Convergence work in Solo mode?
evals.expected_output: Quality Gate (Phase 5): 7 dimensions — (1) Completeness: all 4 mandatory sections present; (2) Decision Coverage: >= 3 concrete decisions; (3) Scope Precision: <= 5 scope items, each actionable; (4) Constraint Clarity: >= 2 constraints classified; (5) Advisor Convergence: all advisors align (auto-pass for Solo); (6) Round Depth: >= 2 rounds with new insights; (7) Handoff Readiness: enough specificity for devise. Quality tiers: 7/7 Excellent (auto-suggest devise), 5-6/7 Good (suggest handoff, mention failing dimensions), 3-4/7 Developing (suggest another round), 0-2/7 Early (continue brainstorming). Advisor Convergence auto-passes in Solo mode since there are no advisors to disagree. Results written to workspace-meta.json.
expectations: Lists all 7 dimensions with pass conditions
Notes Advisor Convergence auto-passes in Solo mode
Lists 4 quality tiers: Excellent, Good, Developing, Early
Maps tiers to score ranges: 7/7, 5-6/7, 3-4/7, 0-2/7
Notes Excellent tier auto-suggests devise
Mentions results written to workspace-meta.json

evals.prompt: Walk me through Phase 6 Cleanup and Handoff. What are the 5 cleanup components? What is the fallback array for brainstorm? What handoff options are presented?
evals.expected_output: Phase 6 Cleanup and Handoff: Output written to TWO locations: tmp/brainstorm-{timestamp}/brainstorm-decisions.md (workspace) and docs/brainstorms/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-brainstorm.md (persistent). Cleanup (Team/Deep modes) follows the standard 5-component pattern: (1) Dynamic member discovery from team config; (2) shutdown_request to all members; (3) Grace period (20s); (4) TeamDelete retry-with-backoff (4 attempts: 0s, 3s, 6s, 10s); (5) Filesystem fallback gated on !cleanupTeamDeleteSucceeded. Fallback array: user-advocate, tech-realist, devils-advocate, elicitation-sage-1, elicitation-sage-2, elicitation-sage-3, state-weaver. SEC-4 validated. Workflow lock released. Handoff: 4 options via AskUserQuestion — Plan with /rune:devise (Recommended), Refine brainstorm, Ask more questions, Save and stop.
expectations: Notes output written to TWO locations: workspace and docs/brainstorms/
Lists all 5 cleanup components in order
Notes grace period is 20 seconds
Lists fallback array with all 7 possible teammates
Notes SEC-4 validation on member names
Notes QUAL-012: filesystem fallback gated on !cleanupTeamDeleteSucceeded
Lists 4 handoff options including /rune:devise recommendation
Notes workflow lock is released

evals.prompt: I want to run /rune:brainstorm --quick 'improve auth'. What phases are skipped? What does the output look like? How does it differ from roundtable mode?
evals.expected_output: --quick flag forces Solo mode: Skips Phase 1 (Team Bootstrap — no agents spawned), Phase 4 (Elicitation Sages). Understanding Rounds (Phase 2) use AskUserQuestion directly instead of advisor SendMessage. Phase 3 Explore Approaches: Lead proposes 2-3 options directly. Design Asset Detection (Phase 3.5) still runs. Quality Gate (Phase 5) still runs but Advisor Convergence auto-passes. Output: same template structure but omits Advisor Perspectives section, omits Elicitation Insights section. Key difference from Roundtable: no agents spawned, no team created, no cleanup needed, faster execution. Output is same markdown format with YAML frontmatter (mode: solo).
expectations: Notes --quick forces Solo mode
Phase 1 (Team Bootstrap) skipped — no agents
Phase 4 (Elicitation Sages) skipped
Understanding Rounds use AskUserQuestion directly
Quality Gate still runs with auto-pass on Advisor Convergence
Output omits Advisor Perspectives and Elicitation Insights sections
Notes no team created, no cleanup needed
Notes mode: solo in frontmatter

evals.prompt: Explain the brainstorm output template. What sections are mandatory vs conditional? What is the workspace-meta.json structure? How does devise consume brainstorm output?
evals.expected_output: Output template: Mandatory sections (all modes): What We're Building, Chosen Approach (with rationale), Key Constraints, Non-Goals (>= 1 item), Constraint Classification (>= 2 rows), Success Criteria (>= 2 items), Scope Boundary (In-Scope + Out-of-Scope), Open Questions. Conditional sections: Advisor Perspectives (Roundtable/Deep only), Design Assets (when Figma detected), Elicitation Insights (Deep only), State Machine Analysis (Deep, when triggered). YAML frontmatter: title, date, mode, quality_score, quality_tier, workspace, advisors, rounds_completed, approach_selected, devise_ready. workspace-meta.json: mode, feature, advisors, timing, quality tier, config_dir, session_id. Devise consumption: quality_score >= 0.70 = high-confidence; approach_selected becomes research starting point; Non-Goals/Success Criteria/Scope pre-populated into plan; Open Questions guide research agent focus.
expectations: Lists all 8 mandatory sections
Lists 4 conditional sections with trigger conditions
Lists YAML frontmatter fields
Describes workspace-meta.json contents
Notes quality_score >= 0.70 triggers high-confidence in devise
Notes approach_selected becomes research starting point
Notes Non-Goals/Success Criteria/Scope pre-populated into plan

evals.prompt: What error handling exists in brainstorm? Explain what happens when: (1) An advisor times out, (2) A sage times out, (3) TeamCreate fails, (4) Echo search is unavailable, (5) Figma MCP is unavailable.
evals.expected_output: Error handling matrix: (1) Advisor timeout (>advisorTimeoutMs, default 90s): proceed with available responses — don't block on one slow advisor. (2) Sage timeout (>5 min): proceed without sage output. (3) TeamCreate failure: catch-and-recover via teamTransition protocol (TeamDelete retry + filesystem fallback + 'Already leading' recovery). (4) Echo search unavailable: skip auto-detection, start fresh brainstorm. (5) Figma MCP unavailable: skip design inventory, proceed without design section. Also: State-weaver timeout (>60s): proceed silently. No matching brainstorm found: start fresh. TeamDelete failure during cleanup: retry-with-backoff, filesystem fallback.
expectations: Advisor timeout: proceed with available responses
Sage timeout: proceed without sage output
TeamCreate failure: catch-and-recover via teamTransition
Echo search unavailable: skip auto-detection, start fresh
Figma MCP unavailable: skip design inventory
State-weaver timeout: proceed silently
TeamDelete cleanup failure: retry-with-backoff + filesystem fallback

evals.prompt: I ran /rune:brainstorm --deep 'redesign the API layer'. Walk me through the full pipeline: which phases run, how many agents are spawned, and what the final output structure looks like.
evals.expected_output: Deep mode full pipeline: Phase 0 (--deep skips mode choice). Phase 1 Team Bootstrap: creates rune-brainstorm-{timestamp} team, spawns 3 advisor agents (user-advocate, tech-realist, devils-advocate). Phase 2 Understanding Rounds: 2-4 advisor rounds with user interaction. Phase 3 Explore Approaches: advisors propose/challenge, user selects. Phase 3.5 Design Asset Detection: Figma URL scan with SSRF defense. Phase 4 Elicitation Sages: 1-3 sages based on keyword fan-out (15 keywords). Phase 4.5 State Machine Pre-Validation: RESERVED (skipped). Phase 5 Quality Gate: 7-dimension checklist. Phase 6 Capture + Cleanup + Handoff. Total agents: 3 advisors + 1-3 sages = 4-6 agents. Output: brainstorm-decisions.md with all sections including Advisor Perspectives AND Elicitation Insights.
expectations: Notes --deep skips mode choice
Lists all phases that run including Phase 4 Elicitation
Notes 3 advisor agents spawned in Phase 1
Notes 1-3 elicitation sages in Phase 4
Total agents: 4-6 (3 advisors + 1-3 sages)
Notes Phase 4.5 is RESERVED (skipped)
Output includes both Advisor Perspectives AND Elicitation Insights
Notes SSRF defense in Phase 3.5

evals.prompt: What are the 3 brainstorm mode type prefixes in workspace-meta.json? Are there 3 or 6 types? How does mode affect the output template?
evals.expected_output: There are exactly 3 brainstorm mode types: 'solo', 'roundtable', 'deep'. These are the ONLY valid values for the mode field in both workspace-meta.json and the brainstorm output YAML frontmatter. There are NOT 6 types — the 3 modes map to: Solo (--quick flag, no agents), Roundtable (default team mode, 3 advisors), Deep (--deep flag, advisors + sages). Mode affects output template: Solo omits Advisor Perspectives and Elicitation Insights sections. Roundtable includes Advisor Perspectives but omits Elicitation Insights. Deep includes both Advisor Perspectives AND Elicitation Insights (plus State Machine Analysis when triggered).
expectations: States exactly 3 mode types: solo, roundtable, deep
Explicitly notes there are NOT 6 types
Maps modes to flags: solo=--quick, deep=--deep, roundtable=default
Solo omits Advisor Perspectives and Elicitation Insights
Roundtable includes Advisor Perspectives, omits Elicitation Insights
Deep includes Advisor Perspectives AND Elicitation Insights

evals.prompt: What talisman configuration options affect brainstorm? How are defaults resolved? What happens when the misc section is absent?
evals.expected_output: Talisman brainstorm config lives under misc.brainstorm: advisor_timeout_ms (default 90000), max_rounds (default 4), advisor_word_limit (default 300). Read via readTalismanSection('misc'). When misc section is absent: all defaults apply (90s timeout, 4 rounds, 300 words). Merge order: defaults < global talisman < project talisman. Configuration is non-blocking — missing talisman doesn't prevent brainstorm from running.
expectations: Lists 3 config options: advisor_timeout_ms, max_rounds, advisor_word_limit
Lists defaults: 90000ms, 4 rounds, 300 words
Notes config lives under misc.brainstorm
Notes readTalismanSection('misc') is used
Notes all defaults apply when misc section is absent
Notes configuration is non-blocking

evals.prompt: What happens when a brainstorm already exists for 'auth-flow' and I run /rune:brainstorm 'improve auth flow'? How does slug matching and echo search work together?
evals.expected_output: Brainstorm auto-detection: Scans 3 locations (docs/brainstorms/*.md, tmp/plans/*/brainstorm-decisions.md, tmp/brainstorm-*/brainstorm-decisions.md). Slug matching: extracts slug from filename (e.g., 'auth-flow' from '2026-03-01-auth-flow-brainstorm.md'), matches when feature description slug appears in filename slug. 'improve auth flow' contains 'auth-flow' — match found. Recency filter: skip files older than 90 days. Echo search: also queries echo_search MCP for related brainstorm entries. Both results presented together. If match found: AskUserQuestion to confirm reuse — NEVER auto-use. User decides: reuse existing context or start fresh.
expectations: Lists all 3 scan locations
Explains slug extraction from filenames
Shows 'auth-flow' slug would match
Notes 90-day recency filter
Notes echo_search also queried in parallel
Notes AskUserQuestion required — NEVER auto-use
User can choose reuse or start fresh

evals.prompt: How does brainstorm handle the workflow lock? What lock type is used? What happens on conflict with an active arc or review?
evals.expected_output: Workflow lock: brainstorm acquires a 'writer' lock via rune_check_conflicts/rune_acquire_lock from workflow-lock.sh. Writer lock type means it conflicts with other writer locks (arc, strive, mend) but NOT with reader locks (review, audit). On conflict: reports the holder workflow and lock type via AskUserQuestion — user decides whether to proceed. QUAL-303: also checks for advisory signals (tmp/.rune-signals/advisory-*.json) but doesn't block (brainstorm is low-risk). Lock released in Phase 6 cleanup via rune_release_lock.
expectations: Notes 'writer' lock type
Describes conflict detection via rune_check_conflicts
Notes writer conflicts with other writers but not readers
Notes AskUserQuestion on conflict — user decides
Notes QUAL-303 advisory signal check (non-blocking)
Notes lock released in Phase 6 cleanup
